project_structure: Fabric CLI monorepo (pnpm workspaces). Three packages: @fenglimg/fabric-cli (packages/cli — citty-based CLI surface, command modules under src/commands/), @fenglimg/fabric-shared (packages/shared — i18n translator + locale message tables + protected-tokens registry), @fenglimg/fabric-server (packages/server — MCP HTTP service, also exposes checkLockOrThrow consumed by lock-sensitive CLI commands). The CLI entrypoint (packages/cli/src/index.ts) builds a citty defineCommand 'fabric' with subCommands sourced from the allCommands registry in packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts (lazy dynamic imports). Each subcommand is a defineCommand module exporting `default` (the command) and helper functions; meta.description and args[].description strings are resolved through t() at module-load time. Tests live under packages/cli/__tests__/, with snapshots in __tests__/__snapshots__/ — the public surface is pinned by cli-surface.test.ts (drift gate). Documentation seed is docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 (Feature Surface) which lists the public command set and flags. Release notes follow Keep-a-Changelog conventions in CHANGELOG.md.

relevant_files.rationale: The canonical subcommand registry consumed by citty's subCommands wiring. Adding `uninstall` requires inserting a new lazy-import entry here, symmetric to `init`. This is the single integration point that surfaces a new top-level `fab uninstall`.

key_code.description: Lazy dynamic-import map keyed by command name. Each value is a thunk returning the default export of the command module. Adding `uninstall: () => import('./uninstall.js').then((m) => m.default)` is the minimal wiring change.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: this file is THE registration touchpoint — without an entry here, citty will not surface `fab uninstall` regardless of how complete the uninstall.ts module is.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI entrypoint that wires allCommands into citty's defineCommand. Read to confirm no per-command registration is required at the entrypoint level (i.e., adding to allCommands is sufficient).

key_code.description: defineCommand({ meta: { name:'fabric', version, description }, subCommands: allCommands }). Subcommands come from the registry — no further wiring needed when adding `uninstall`.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: confirms the registration model is centralized in commands/index.ts; no entrypoint edits required for a new top-level command.

relevant_files.rationale: The symmetric counterpart that `uninstall` must mirror. Demonstrates: defineCommand args schema with t() descriptions, runInitCommand pattern, --target/--debug/--force/--yes/--plan flag conventions, lock preflight via checkLockOrThrow when needed, intent-resolution + plan-builder + executor 3-phase pattern, writeStderr for warnings, paint.muted footer, FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE handling. Almost every constraint on uninstall's shape comes from matching this file's idioms.

key_code.description: Canonical citty defineCommand shape: meta.name/meta.description from t('cli.init.description'), args block with type/description/default/negativeDescription (all description strings flow through t() at module-load time so locale is captured deterministically), async run({ args }) delegates to runInitCommand(args).

Three-phase orchestrator: resolveInitCliIntent (parse args + env) → buildInitExecutionPlan (pure plan) → executeInitExecutionPlan (effects). Includes checkLockOrThrow preflight for --reapply, compat warnings via writeStderr+t(), and a paint.muted footer line. This is the pattern uninstall should mirror.

Materializes paths the command will touch: .fabric/, knowledge subdirs, AGENTS.md, agents.meta.json, events.jsonl, forensic.json, personal mirror under FABRIC_HOME ?? homedir(). For uninstall, this list is the canonical inventory of what to remove (team root only — personal root must NOT be auto-removed, see clarification_needs).
['decisions','pitfalls','guidelines','models','processes','pending'] — the canonical knowledge subdir list. Uninstall must remove these (or the whole .fabric/knowledge/ tree).

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: init.ts is the structural template for uninstall.ts. Every flag, every i18n key prefix, every test scaffold, every snapshot field — uninstall is expected to be the inverse-shaped sibling of this file.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI-local i18n entry — `t` is imported here from @fenglimg/fabric-shared and re-exported with locale captured at module load. All uninstall description/output strings will route through this same `t()` so they must have keys defined in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN}.ts.

key_code.description: createTranslator(locale) — locale is resolved once at module load via detectNodeLocale(). New cli.uninstall.* keys must exist in both locale tables before uninstall.ts can import this module without missing-key warnings.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: every user-visible string in uninstall must hit this translator. The integration touchpoint is implicit but binding — uninstall.ts cannot ship without a parallel cli.uninstall.* key set.

relevant_files.rationale: English message table. The `cli.init.*` key family (cli.init.description, cli.init.args.*.description, cli.init.errors.*, cli.init.stages.*, cli.init.plan.*, cli.init.mode.*, cli.init.compat.*) defines the convention uninstall must mirror as cli.uninstall.*. Adding/missing-key behavior is enforced by the i18n.test.ts snapshot.

key_code.description: Keys follow `cli.<command>.<subdomain>.<facet>` convention: cli.init.description (command help), cli.init.args.<flag>.description (flag help), cli.init.args.no-<flag>.description (negativeDescription), cli.init.errors.abort-existing (errors), cli.init.plan.* (plan-mode output), cli.init.mode.badge.* (mode banners). Uninstall must add cli.uninstall.* with parallel coverage.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: the i18n message table is a registration point in disguise — keys must exist before the command module loads, or t() falls back to the raw key string and the i18n snapshot drifts. This is a hard integration constraint.

relevant_files.rationale: Chinese message table. Must contain matching cli.uninstall.* keys; i18n.test.ts snapshot enforces parity across en and zh-CN locales by rendering both.

key_code.description: '在目标项目中初始化 Fabric。' — sets the translation register (concise, command-line voice). cli.uninstall.description should match this style, e.g. '从目标项目中卸载 Fabric。'

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: zh-CN parity is the other half of the i18n integration contract. Missing zh-CN keys causes locale-rendered output to fall back to en or to raw key strings, breaking snapshot tests.

relevant_files.rationale: Registry of strings that must survive translation verbatim (MCP tool names, .fabric/ paths, hard-rule keywords). Uninstall messages reference paths like `.fabric/knowledge/` and `.fabric/events.jsonl` which are already in the registry — no new tokens needed, but uninstall message text MUST use these exact strings.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: a constraint-flavored touchpoint — uninstall i18n strings that name protected paths must use the exact registry forms, enforced by scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI public-surface drift gate. Currently imports init/scan/doctor/serve and asserts the public command set is EXACTLY {init,scan,doctor,serve}. Adding `uninstall` will break the equality assertion on line 99 (`expect(names).toEqual(['doctor','init','scan','serve'])`) and require a new it.each row + snapshot + critical-flag assertions for uninstall.

key_code.description: Hard equality on the sorted command names. MUST be updated to include 'uninstall' or the test fails. This is the most rigid integration point — the assertion exists precisely to flag command-surface changes.
Each command surface is snapshotted. Adding uninstall requires a new row in the it.each table and a new snapshot block in cli-surface.test.ts.snap.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: this is the gating test for adding ANY new public command. Until this test is updated, `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test` fails — making it the most concrete integration checkpoint.

relevant_files.rationale: Snapshot pinned by cli-surface.test.ts. Adding `uninstall` requires regenerating with `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u` to append a `fab uninstall surface` snapshot block.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: derivative of cli-surface.test.ts — must be regenerated, not hand-edited.

relevant_files.rationale: i18n.test.ts collects rendered descriptions for init/scan/doctor/serve in both en and zh-CN and snapshots them. Adding uninstall + collecting it in collectSnapshots() will require regenerating this snapshot.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: registers uninstall in the i18n drift gate. Without an entry in collectSnapshots(), uninstall's translations are not protected from silent drift.

relevant_files.rationale: i18n drift gate test. collectSnapshots() (L49+) instantiates each public command and renders its description in both locales. Uninstall must be added to this collection or its translations escape verification.

key_code.description: Instantiates each command under FAB_LANG=<locale> and aggregates rendered descriptions into a single snapshot. The function body would need an `await import('../src/commands/uninstall.ts')` and a corresponding row in the collected object.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: the i18n parity drift gate. Wiring uninstall in here is what guarantees the new translations are exercised in CI.

relevant_files.rationale: Shared test fixture utilities (createWerewolfFixtureRoot copies __tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub, setProcessTty, cleanupFixtureRoot). Uninstall tests must reuse these — a fresh fixture should be `init`-ed first, then uninstall executed against the resulting tree.

key_code.description: Copies packages/cli/__tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub to a tmpdir, strips pre-existing AGENTS.md/.fabric/.claude so the fixture starts clean. Uninstall tests will need a variant that runs `fab init` first to produce realistic .fabric/ state to remove.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: defines the test-fixture scaffold uninstall tests must use to stay consistent with init's test conventions.

relevant_files.rationale: Canonical test fixture (a cocos2d/JS stub project) used by createWerewolfFixtureRoot. Uninstall tests will operate against an init-ed copy of this fixture.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: the substrate uninstall tests will mutate; no changes needed to the fixture itself.

relevant_files.rationale: Test seed document §1 Feature Surface enumerates the public command set and flag inventory. cli-surface.test.ts references this seed in its DRIFT_HINT — adding uninstall requires updating §1 with a `uninstall —` bullet listing flags, plus likely a new behavioral invariant (I-row) and possibly a TASK-pin row capturing the symmetry contract with init.

key_code.description: Four bullets, one per public command, listing flags. Adding `- uninstall — ...; flags: --target, --debug, --force, --yes, --plan, --keep-knowledge?, --personal?` is required before merging — otherwise the drift gate's DRIFT_HINT pointer is stale.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: the human-readable surface contract. The drift test points at this file by name — keeping it in sync is part of the integration protocol.

relevant_files.rationale: Top-level README contains a quick-reference command list (`fabric init`, `fabric serve`, `fabric doctor` at L202-L205) and references docs/initialization.md. Adding uninstall likely warrants a parallel line + cross-link to a docs/uninstall.md (if scope requires it).

key_code.description: `fabric init  # install hooks + Skills + bootstrap` / `fabric serve  # start the MCP server` / `fabric doctor  # run all 25 lints, report only` / `fabric doctor --apply-lint  # apply auto-fixable lints`. Adding `fabric uninstall  # remove Fabric-managed artifacts` fits the same style.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: docs-side integration touchpoint. Lower-priority than code/test integration but required for discoverability.

relevant_files.rationale: Keep-a-Changelog format. New features land under `### Added` for the next rc bump (currently rc.8 was the last published; uninstall would target rc.9 or stable). Entries follow `**short title**: detailed bullet` pattern with file:line references.

key_code.description: Header `## [2.0.0-rc.8] — 2026-05-13`, lead paragraph in bold for theme, `### Fixed` / `### Added` / `### Changed` subsections, bullets reference exact file paths and rationale. Uninstall entry should follow this voice and pin paths.

relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: release-note integration touchpoint. The rc cadence (rc.8 most recent) is the relevant variable for scope (clarification_needs).

relevant_files.rationale: Read briefly to confirm flag-and-help conventions are stable across commands (target/debug/force shared). Uninstall need only re-validate consistency.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: secondary reference for citty defineCommand style (init.ts is the primary).

relevant_files.rationale: Exports checkLockOrThrow (consumed by init when --reapply is used). If uninstall needs to refuse running while serve holds the lock, it must call checkLockOrThrow similarly. Lock surface is an integration point for any state-mutating command.
relevant_files.topic_relation: integration-points exploration: lock-coordination touchpoint. Uninstall removes .fabric/ which contains the lockfile, so lock-respecting preflight is structurally important.

patterns: 1) Subcommand registration: append to allCommands map in packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts; each entry is `name: () => import('./<name>.js').then((m) => m.default)`. No edits to packages/cli/src/index.ts required. 2) Command module shape: `export const <name>Command = defineCommand({ meta: { name, description: t('cli.<name>.description') }, args: { ... t('cli.<name>.args.<flag>.description') ... }, async run({ args }) { await run<Name>Command(args); } }); export default <name>Command;`. The args object uses citty types (string/boolean), with `description`, `default`, and for booleans `negativeDescription` for --no-<flag>. 3) Three-phase orchestrator: resolve<Name>CliIntent(args) → build<Name>ExecutionPlan(intent) → execute<Name>ExecutionPlan(plan). Plan is a pure data structure; execution does effects. 4) i18n keys: every user-visible string uses t('cli.<command>.<facet>'); en + zh-CN parallel coverage; protected paths (`.fabric/...`, `AGENTS.md`) must remain verbatim. 5) Lock awareness: lock-sensitive commands call checkLockOrThrow(target, { force: args.force }) in preflight (see init.ts L313-L315). 6) Output style: success via console.log + paint.success/muted; warnings/errors via process.stderr.write through writeStderr() helper; clack prompts (confirm/select/group) for interactive flows, gated behind isInteractiveInit() + FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE != '1'. 7) Test conventions: cli-surface.test.ts snapshots command args (sorted); i18n.test.ts cross-renders both locales; init-test-utils.ts provides createWerewolfFixtureRoot for tmpdir fixtures derived from __tests__/fixtures/cocos-stub. 8) Snapshot regeneration: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u`. 9) Atomic writes (for symmetry — uninstall mostly removes, but if it writes a `.fabric/.uninstall-receipt` etc.): atomicWriteText/atomicWriteJson from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write.
dependencies: Workspace internal: @fenglimg/fabric-shared (createTranslator, detectNodeLocale, atomicWriteJson, atomicWriteText, AgentsMeta, defaultAgentsMetaCounters, PROTECTED_TOKENS); @fenglimg/fabric-server (checkLockOrThrow — only if uninstall must respect the serve-lock). Local imports inside packages/cli/src: ../i18n.js (t), ../colors.js (paint, displayWidth, padEnd), ../dev-mode.js (createDebugLogger, resolveDevMode), ../config/json.js (ClaudeMcpScope — only if MCP entries must be unwound from .mcp.json / ~/.claude.json), ../install/skills-and-hooks.js (only if hooks/skills require explicit removal rather than rm-rf of .fabric/). External (already in package.json): citty (defineCommand), @clack/prompts (intro/confirm/group/note/log/outro/isCancel — only for interactive uninstall confirm). Node built-ins: node:fs (rmSync, existsSync), node:path (join, resolve), node:os (homedir for FABRIC_HOME fallback).
integration_points:
FILE-LEVEL TOUCHPOINTS (in dependency order):

1. packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts:1-9 — append `uninstall: () => import('./uninstall.js').then((module) => module.default),` to allCommands. Mirrors init at L2.

2. packages/cli/src/commands/uninstall.ts (NEW) — defineCommand mirroring initCommand (packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:234-302). Suggested args parity: --target, --debug, --force, --yes, --plan, optionally --keep-knowledge, --keep-events, --personal (to also strip ~/.fabric/knowledge/ — see clarification_needs).

3. packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts — add cli.uninstall.* key family alongside cli.init.* (currently L98-L207). Required keys: cli.uninstall.description, cli.uninstall.args.target.description, cli.uninstall.args.debug.description, cli.uninstall.args.force.description, cli.uninstall.args.yes.description, cli.uninstall.args.plan.description, plus cli.uninstall.errors.*, cli.uninstall.plan.*, cli.uninstall.stages.*, cli.uninstall.confirm.prompt etc. as the executor structure demands.

4. packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts — add matching cli.uninstall.* keys (Chinese parallel of #3).

5. packages/cli/__tests__/cli-surface.test.ts:12-15 — add `import uninstallCommand from '../src/commands/uninstall.ts';` and append a row to the it.each table (L76-L86). Critical: L92-L100 hard-equality `expect(names).toEqual(['doctor','init','scan','serve'])` MUST be updated to include 'uninstall' (sort yields ['doctor','init','scan','serve','uninstall']).

6. packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/cli-surface.test.ts.snap — regenerate via `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -u` to append the `fab uninstall surface` snapshot block.

7. packages/cli/__tests__/i18n.test.ts:49+ collectSnapshots — add `await import('../src/commands/uninstall.ts')` and include uninstall description in the collected object so both locales' uninstall text is snapshotted.

8. packages/cli/__tests__/__snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap — regenerate after #7.

9. docs/test-seed/cli.md:10-13 §1 Feature Surface — add `- uninstall — 从项目中移除 Fabric-managed 资产；flags: ...` bullet. Optionally add an I-row behavioral invariant (e.g. I11: `uninstall` 必须幂等：连续两次产生一致的退出码与最终文件系统状态) and a TASK-row for the symmetric-with-init invariant.

10. README.md:202-205 — add `fabric uninstall  # remove Fabric-managed artifacts (knowledge stays unless --purge)` line under the quick-reference block.

11. CHANGELOG.md — add `## [2.0.0-rc.9] — <date>` (or stable, per release scope) with `### Added` bullet citing this exploration's session folder and the new files. Pattern matches the rc.8 entry at L8-L36.

12. (Optional) docs/uninstall.md (NEW) — symmetric companion to docs/initialization.md, cross-linked from README.md:267-style.

13. (Optional) packages/cli/src/commands/uninstall.ts preflight may call `checkLockOrThrow(target, { force: args.force })` (mirroring init.ts:313-315) so uninstall refuses to run while a serve process holds the lock — this matches docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 I10 invariant.

Non-touchpoints (verified): packages/cli/src/index.ts (no edit needed — registry-driven), packages/server/* (no server-side state to migrate — uninstall removes client-side .fabric/ only).
constraints:
TEST GATES (must update or break): (a) cli-surface.test.ts L92-L100 hard-equality assertion on public command set — this is the most rigid constraint and the test fails before any other coverage runs. (b) cli-surface.test.ts.snap — auto-regenerated but the change must be reviewed for unexpected drift. (c) i18n.test.ts collectSnapshots must include uninstall or the new translations escape parity verification. (d) lint-protected-tokens.test.ts — uninstall i18n strings referencing `.fabric/`, `AGENTS.md`, `.fabric/knowledge/`, `.fabric/events.jsonl`, `.fabric/agents.meta.json` must use the exact registry forms from packages/shared/src/i18n/protected-tokens.ts (no translation/paraphrasing).

I18N PARITY: every cli.uninstall.* key MUST exist in both en.ts and zh-CN.ts; the i18n.test.ts snapshot fails on missing keys (translator returns the raw key string, which then appears in the snapshot diff).

DESIGN SYMMETRY: init.ts is structurally complex (~1590 lines: wizard, capability table, MCP install modes, lock preflight, post-setup hand-off prompt). Uninstall does not need full symmetry — but the THREE-PHASE intent → plan → execute orchestrator pattern, the --plan dry-run flag, and the --force / FABRIC_NONINTERACTIVE / interactive-TTY contracts are load-bearing conventions the codebase enforces by test and by convention.

NON-DESTRUCTIVE DEFAULT: init refuses to clobber by default (planFreshPath throws 'cli.init.errors.abort-existing' unless --force). Uninstall is the mirror image — by default it SHOULD refuse if .fabric/ does not exist (no-op safety), and it SHOULD prompt or refuse before deleting if .fabric/events.jsonl contains entries that would be lost. Reapply-style force semantics need mapping: `fab uninstall --force` = bypass safety prompts; `fab uninstall --plan` = dry run.

PROTECTED PATHS: .fabric/ is in PROTECTED_TOKENS (cannot be paraphrased in messages); ~/.fabric/knowledge/ (personal mirror under FABRIC_HOME ?? homedir()) is created by init but uninstall removing it is potentially destructive across multiple repos — strongly suggests an opt-in --personal flag rather than default removal.

LOCK SAFETY: docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 I10 — lock-sensitive commands refuse to run while another process holds the serve-lock unless --force; uninstall removes .fabric/ which contains the lockfile, so locking semantics must be deliberate (refuse → release → remove vs. force → remove).

NO BREAKING INIT TESTS: init's test suite (init-cli-surface.test.ts, init-atomic.test.ts, init-wizard.test.ts, init-wizard-adapter.test.ts, integration/init-*.test.ts, init-forensic.test.ts, init-context-shadow-mirroring.test.ts) MUST continue to pass unchanged — uninstall must not refactor init's plan-builder signatures.

RELEASE PIPELINE: per project memory, v2.0.0 RC chain is ongoing (rc.8 most recent, never published to npm before rc.8). New feature → rc bump; the rc.8 publish-pipeline fix (apply-tag-version.mjs, sync-versions.mjs --tag) is the relevant CI surface for the next bump.

clarification_needs.question: What is the scope of `fab uninstall`'s default removal — should it remove only Fabric-managed scaffold (`.fabric/agents.meta.json`, `.fabric/forensic.json`, `.fabric/events.jsonl`, .fabric/.import-requested sentinel) while PRESERVING `.fabric/knowledge/` (user knowledge entries), or should it default to removing the entire `.fabric/` directory tree?
clarification_needs.context: Init creates two categories of state: (a) derived scaffold the user did not author (agents.meta, forensic.json, empty events.jsonl, .gitkeep markers) and (b) user-authored knowledge entries under .fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes,pending}/ which accumulate over time. Removing (b) by default is destructive of user work; preserving (b) by default makes uninstall less symmetric with init but safer. The init code at packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:573-617 enumerates both categories. Once-decided, this flips the default for --keep-knowledge vs --purge-knowledge framing.
options: Default preserve .fabric/knowledge/; remove only derived scaffold (safest, asymmetric with init) — add --purge flag to also remove knowledge
Default remove all of .fabric/ team root, but leave ~/.fabric/knowledge/ personal root (symmetric with init's team-root focus) — add --keep-knowledge to bail on knowledge tree
Default remove all of .fabric/ team root AND ~/.fabric/knowledge/ personal root (fully symmetric inverse of init)
Refuse to run unless an explicit scope flag (--scope scaffold|knowledge|all) is given (no implicit default)

clarification_needs.question: What is the scope of related docs/CHANGELOG updates required for this feature — minimal (CHANGELOG entry + test-seed §1 bullet only), moderate (also README.md quick-ref line), or comprehensive (also a new docs/uninstall.md companion to docs/initialization.md)?
clarification_needs.context: Init has multiple documentation surfaces: docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 (test seed, required for the drift gate to stay coherent), docs/initialization.md (cross-linked from README.md:267 'what fabric init does'), README.md L202-L205 quick-reference, and CHANGELOG.md per-rc entries. Per project memory, v2.0.0 RC chain continues toward rc.9 / stable; this feature would ship in a new rc tag. The doc scope decision affects how many files the implementation PR touches and whether docs/uninstall.md needs to exist for parity.
options: Minimal: CHANGELOG.md entry + docs/test-seed/cli.md §1 bullet (+ test-seed I/T invariants for behavioral pins) — bare minimum for drift-gate coherence
Moderate: above + README.md L202-L205 quick-ref line — minimal-with-discoverability
Comprehensive: above + new docs/uninstall.md symmetric to docs/initialization.md + cross-link in README.md:267-style
Defer: ship code + tests + CHANGELOG only, file a follow-up issue for docs/uninstall.md

clarification_needs.question: Should `fab uninstall` also unwind side-effect installations performed by `fab init` outside the project (MCP server entries in `.mcp.json` / `~/.claude.json`, Stop hook entries in `.claude/settings.json` / `.codex/hooks/...`, Skill template directories under `.claude/skills/fabric-*` / `.codex/skills/fabric-*`, Cursor `.cursor/rules` pointer line, AGENTS.md content)?
clarification_needs.context: Init's bootstrap stage (packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts:852-891) installs Skills + hooks + per-client hook configs + an AGENTS.md anchor + a CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules pointer line via installFabricArchiveSkill / installFabricReviewSkill / installFabricImportSkill / mergeClaudeCodeHookConfig / mergeCodexHookConfig / mergeCursorHookConfig / addArchiveSkillPointer. Init's MCP stage (L892-L915) installs MCP server entries. AGENTS.md is preserved if present (idempotent) — uninstall must also be conservative here. Full inverse parity vs. .fabric-only removal is a major scope choice.
options: Conservative: only remove .fabric/ (team root); leave Skills/hooks/MCP/AGENTS.md untouched — user manually unwinds external state

Full inverse: also remove Skills + hooks + MCP entries + the pointer line in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules — preserve AGENTS.md if it has non-default content (mirror init's preserve-if-present rule)
Staged: default conservative behavior + --deep flag that adds Skills/hooks/MCP/pointer-line removal
Refuse to remove anything outside .fabric/ — instruct user via output to manually uninstall client assets

clarification_needs.question: Which release vehicle should the `fab uninstall` feature target — the next rc bump (rc.9) or wait for v2.0.0 stable / v2.1.0?
clarification_needs.context: Project memory: v2.0.0 was tagged locally but never published; rc.8 is the most recently published rc and rolled up rc.2–rc.7. The release pipeline was just stabilized in rc.8 (apply-tag-version.mjs, sync-versions.mjs --tag). CHANGELOG.md uses Keep-a-Changelog with explicit rc tags. Whether uninstall lands in an rc or in stable affects entry placement, surface stability commitments, and whether it needs a deprecation window if its flags change.
options: rc.9 (next): ship as `### Added` under a new `## [2.0.0-rc.9]` entry — fastest path, accepts iteration risk
v2.0.0 stable (rollup): include in the eventual stable release entry alongside other RC work — slower, more polished
v2.1.0 minor: treat as post-stable enhancement — most conservative, but delays the feature

_metadata.task_description: Execute integration-points exploration: where exactly an `uninstall` command must hook into the existing CLI surface. Add `fab uninstall` symmetric to `fab init`.
